task_completion.description: The Descriptives analysis panel is open in Jamovi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Descriptives' settings panel is visible on the left side of the Jamovi window.

task_completion.description: The 'extra' variable is assigned to the 'Variables' box.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Descriptives settings panel, the 'extra' variable is listed inside the 'Variables' box.

task_completion.description: The 'group' variable is assigned to the 'Split by' box.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Descriptives settings panel, the 'group' variable is listed inside the 'Split by' box.

task_completion.description: The results table displays the required statistics for the 'extra' variable, split by 'group'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output panel on the right shows a 'Descriptives' table containing Mean, Median, Standard deviation, Minimum, and Maximum. There should be separate columns or rows showing these values for group 1 and group 2.

task_completion.description: A histogram for the data is generated and visible in the results panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output panel on the right displays a histogram plot for the 'extra' variable. It should show the distribution split or faceted by 'group'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the Jamovi application interface (menus, drag-and-drop, checkboxes), rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The results appear in the native Jamovi output panel.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output tables and plots are rendered in Jamovi's distinct style on the right side of the application window, not pasted as text or images from an external source.

integrity.description: No external statistical software or text editors were used to generate the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Only Jamovi is used for the analysis; no other tools like RStudio, Python, or text editors are visible generating the outputs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 20 observations total, with 10 participants measured twice. The 'group' variable has two levels (1 and 2). The output table should show statistics calculated separately for group 1 and group 2. The histogram should also reflect the split by group.
